title: eschweilera pachyderma
intro: Eschweilera pachyderma, also known as the porcupine tree or porcupine bush, is a species of flowering plant in the family Lecythidaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the leaves and bark of Eschweilera pachyderma are often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions that can provide health benefits such as reducing inflammation, relieving coughs, and promoting digestion.
